**Hema Malini Dharmendra Deol**[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) (born 16 October 1948;[[5]](#cite_note-birth-5) Hindi pronunciation: [[ɦeːmaː maːlɪn̪iː d̪ʱəɾmeːn̪d̪ɾə d̪eːoːl]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Hindi_and_Urdu "Help:IPA/Hindi and Urdu")) is an Indian actress, director, producer, and politician who is currently serving as a [member of the Lok Sabha](/wiki/Member_of_the_Lok_Sabha "Member of the Lok Sabha") from the [Bharatiya Janata Party](/wiki/Bharatiya_Janata_Party "Bharatiya Janata Party") (BJP), representing [Mathura](/wiki/Mathura_Lok_Sabha_constituency "Mathura Lok Sabha constituency") constituency since 2014. She was a [member of the Rajya Sabha from Karnataka](/wiki/List_of_Rajya_Sabha_members_from_Karnataka "List of Rajya Sabha members from Karnataka") from 2011 to 2012, subsequent to her nomination to that chamber from 2003 to 2009 as a member of the BJP.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Primarily known for her work in [Hindi](/wiki/Hindi "Hindi") films, she has starred in both comic and dramatic roles, and is one of the most popular and successful leading actresses of mainstream [Hindi cinema](/wiki/Hindi_cinema "Hindi cinema").[[7]](#cite_note-top_actresses-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-Hema-9)

Malini made her acting debut in 1963 with the [Tamil film](/wiki/Tamil_film "Tamil film") *[Idhu Sathiyam](/wiki/Idhu_Sathiyam "Idhu Sathiyam")*.[[10]](#cite_note-10) Malini first acted in a lead role in *[Sapno Ka Saudagar](/wiki/Sapno_Ka_Saudagar "Sapno Ka Saudagar")* (1968), and went on to feature in numerous Hindi films, frequently opposite [Dharmendra](/wiki/Dharmendra "Dharmendra"), whom she married in 1980.[[11]](#cite_note-newindianexpress-11)[[12]](#cite_note-Rediff_2003-12) Malini was initially promoted as the "Dream Girl", and in 1977 starred in a [film of the same name](/wiki/Dream_Girl_(1977_film) "Dream Girl (1977 film)").[[12]](#cite_note-Rediff_2003-12) She won the [Filmfare Award for Best Actress](/wiki/Filmfare_Award_for_Best_Actress "Filmfare Award for Best Actress") for her [dual role](/wiki/Dual_role "Dual role") in the comedy *[Seeta Aur Geeta](/wiki/Seeta_Aur_Geeta "Seeta Aur Geeta")* (1972), and was nominated ten more times leading up to *[Baghban](/wiki/Baghban_(2003_film) "Baghban (2003 film)")* (2003).[[13]](#cite_note-13) In 2000, Malini won the [Filmfare Lifetime Achievement Award](/wiki/Filmfare_Lifetime_Achievement_Award "Filmfare Lifetime Achievement Award") and in 2019 a [Filmfare Special Award](/wiki/Filmfare_Special_Award "Filmfare Special Award") for 50 Years of Outstanding Contribution to Cinema.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Malini was honoured with the [Padma Shri](/wiki/Padma_Shri "Padma Shri") in 2000, the fourth-highest civilian honour awarded by the [Government of India](/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India").[[15]](#cite_note-padma-15) In 2012, the [Sir Padampat Singhania University](/wiki/Sir_Padampat_Singhania_University "Sir Padampat Singhania University") conferred an honorary doctorate on Malini in recognition of her contribution to [Indian cinema](/wiki/Indian_cinema "Indian cinema").[[16]](#cite_note-Subhash_K_Jha-16) Malini served as chairperson of the [National Film Development Corporation](/wiki/National_Film_Development_Corporation_of_India "National Film Development Corporation of India"). In 2006, Malini received the [Sopori Academy of Music And Performing Arts (SaMaPa)](/wiki/Bhajan_Sopori#Music_Academy,_SaMaPa "Bhajan Sopori") Vitasta award from [Bhajan Sopori](/wiki/Bhajan_Sopori "Bhajan Sopori") in Delhi for her contribution and service to Indian culture and dance. In 2013, she received the [NTR National Award](/wiki/NTR_National_Award "NTR National Award") from the [Government of Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Government_of_Andhra_Pradesh "Government of Andhra Pradesh") for her contribution to Indian cinema.[[17]](#cite_note-17) Malini has been involved with charitable and social ventures. Currently, Malini is also a life member of the [International Society for Krishna Consciousness](/wiki/International_Society_for_Krishna_Consciousness "International Society for Krishna Consciousness") (ISKCON).[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Early life and family

Malini was born in a [Tamil](/wiki/Tamils "Tamils") [Iyengar Brahmin](/wiki/Iyengar "Iyengar")[[19]](#cite_note-19) family to Jaya Lakshmi and VSR Chakravarti Iyengar in [Ammankudi](/wiki/Ammankudi "Ammankudi"), [Madras Province](/wiki/Madras_Province "Madras Province") (now [Tamil Nadu](/wiki/Tamil_Nadu "Tamil Nadu")). She has two elder brothers.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22) She attended the Andhra Mahila Sabha in [Chennai](/wiki/Chennai "Chennai").[[23]](#cite_note-telegraph1-23) She studied at DTEA Mandir Marg until the 11th Standard, after which she pursued her acting career.[[24]](#cite_note-rajyasabha-24) She follows [Hinduism](/wiki/Hinduism "Hinduism").[[25]](#cite_note-25)

Mailni first met [Dharmendra](/wiki/Dharmendra "Dharmendra") at a film premiere in 1968, their first film together was *[Tum Haseen Main Jawaan](/wiki/Tum_Haseen_Main_Jawaan "Tum Haseen Main Jawaan")* (1970) which marked their love story.[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27) They later married on 2 May 1980 having known each other for more than ten years and being in relationship for about six years.[[12]](#cite_note-Rediff_2003-12)[[11]](#cite_note-newindianexpress-11)[[28]](#cite_note-dnaindia-28) Dharmendra already had two sons and two daughters with his first wife Prakash Kaur, two of whom later became [Hindi cinema](/wiki/Hindi_cinema "Hindi cinema") actors – [Sunny Deol](/wiki/Sunny_Deol "Sunny Deol") and [Bobby Deol](/wiki/Bobby_Deol "Bobby Deol"). Malini and Dharmendra had two daughters, [Esha Deol](/wiki/Esha_Deol "Esha Deol") (born 1981)[[29]](#cite_note-29) and Ahana Deol Vohra (born 1985).[[30]](#cite_note-30)[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32)

[Madhoo](/wiki/Madhoo "Madhoo"), who played the female lead in *[Phool Aur Kaante](/wiki/Phool_Aur_Kaante "Phool Aur Kaante")*, *[Roja](/wiki/Roja_(film) "Roja (film)")* and *[Annayya](/wiki/Annayya_(1993_film) "Annayya (1993 film)")*, is Malini's cousin.[[33]](#cite_note-33) Malini's father died in 1978 when she was 30 whereas her mother passed away in 2004 when she was 56.

On 11 June 2015, Malini became a grandmother when her younger daughter, Ahana Deol Vohra, gave birth to her first child, a son, Darien Vohra and twin girls Astraia and Adea Vohra were born on 26 November 2020. On 20 October 2017, her elder daughter, Esha Deol, gave birth to a girl, Radhya Takhtani and another baby girl Miraya Takhtani on 10 June 2019.[[34]](#cite_note-34) Businessman Vaibhav Vohra is her son-in-law and Bharat Takhtani is her ex-son-in-law.

Malini's husband Dharmendra died on 24 November 2025 at the age of 89 following a period of brief illness.[[35]](#cite_note-35) Their association lasted 57 years including 45 years together in marriage untill his death.

## Film career

### 1960–1970 (Early work)

Malini played small roles in *[Pandava Vanavasam](/wiki/Pandava_Vanavasam "Pandava Vanavasam")* (1965) and *[Idhu Sathiyam](/wiki/Idhu_Sathiyam "Idhu Sathiyam")* (1962). In 1968, she was chosen to play the female lead opposite [Raj Kapoor](/wiki/Raj_Kapoor "Raj Kapoor") in *[Sapno Ka Saudagar](/wiki/Sapno_Ka_Saudagar "Sapno Ka Saudagar")*[[36]](#cite_note-dgirl-36) and was promoted as the Dream Girl.[[36]](#cite_note-dgirl-36)

### 1970–1980 (Established actress)

Malini played the lead in *[Johny Mera Naam](/wiki/Johny_Mera_Naam "Johny Mera Naam")* (1970). Roles in subsequent films such as *[Andaz](/wiki/Andaz_(1971_film) "Andaz (1971 film)")* (1971) and *[Lal Patthar](/wiki/Lal_Patthar "Lal Patthar")* (1971)[[36]](#cite_note-dgirl-36) established her as a leading actress. In 1972, she played a double role opposite [Dharmendra](/wiki/Dharmendra "Dharmendra") and [Sanjeev Kumar](/wiki/Sanjeev_Kumar_(actor) "Sanjeev Kumar (actor)") in *[Seeta Aur Geeta](/wiki/Seeta_Aur_Geeta "Seeta Aur Geeta")*,[[37]](#cite_note-seeta-37) which earned her a [Filmfare Best Actress Award](/wiki/Filmfare_Best_Actress_Award "Filmfare Best Actress Award") for the film.[[38]](#cite_note-firstff-38) The list of successful films she starred include *[Sanyasi](/wiki/Sanyasi_(1975_film) "Sanyasi (1975 film)")*, *[Dharmatma](/wiki/Dharmatma "Dharmatma")* and *[Pratigya](/wiki/Pratigya_(1975_film) "Pratigya (1975 film)")*, *[Sholay](/wiki/Sholay "Sholay")*, *[Trishul](/wiki/Trishul_(film) "Trishul (film)")* to name a few.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Malini and Dharmendra have acted together in 28 films including *[Sharafat](/wiki/Sharafat "Sharafat")*, *[Tum Haseen Main Jawan](/wiki/Tum_Haseen_Main_Jawan "Tum Haseen Main Jawan")*, *[Naya Zamana](/wiki/Naya_Zamana_(1971_film) "Naya Zamana (1971 film)")*, *[Raja Jani](/wiki/Raja_Jani "Raja Jani")*, *[Seeta Aur Geeta](/wiki/Seeta_Aur_Geeta "Seeta Aur Geeta")*, *[Patthar Aur Payal](/wiki/Patthar_Aur_Payal "Patthar Aur Payal")*, *[Dost](/wiki/Dost_(1974_film) "Dost (1974 film)")* (1974), *[Sholay](/wiki/Sholay "Sholay")* (1975), *[Charas](/wiki/Charas "Charas")*, *[Jugnu](/wiki/Jugnu_(1973_film) "Jugnu (1973 film)")*, *[Apna Khoon](/wiki/Apna_Khoon "Apna Khoon")*, *[Azaad](/wiki/Azaad_(1978_film) "Azaad (1978 film)")* (1978) and *[Dillagi](/wiki/Dillagi_(1978_film) "Dillagi (1978 film)")* (1978).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Her pairing with [Rajesh Khanna](/wiki/Rajesh_Khanna "Rajesh Khanna") was appreciated in *[Andaz](/wiki/Andaz_(1971_film) "Andaz (1971 film)")* and *[Prem Nagar](/wiki/Prem_Nagar_(1974_film) "Prem Nagar (1974 film)")*. Their subsequent films such as *[Mehbooba](/wiki/Mehbooba_(1976_film) "Mehbooba (1976 film)")* and *[Janta Hawaldar](/wiki/Janta_Hawaldar "Janta Hawaldar")* however, did not do well at the box office.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### 1980–1997 (Continued success)

In the 1980s, Malini continued to star in big budget films such as *[Kranti](/wiki/Kranti "Kranti")*, *[Naseeb](/wiki/Naseeb_(1981_film) "Naseeb (1981 film)")*, *[Satte Pe Satta](/wiki/Satte_Pe_Satta "Satte Pe Satta")* and *[Rajput](/wiki/Rajput_(film) "Rajput (film)")*, most of whom proved to be successful at the box office. She continued to work in heroine-centric roles even after becoming a mother in films such as *[Aandhi Toofan](/wiki/Aandhi_Toofan "Aandhi Toofan")*, *[Durgaa](/wiki/Durgaa "Durgaa")*, *[Ramkali](/wiki/Ramkali "Ramkali")*, *[Sitapur Ki Geeta](/wiki/Sitapur_Ki_Geeta "Sitapur Ki Geeta")*, *[Ek Chadar Maili Si](/wiki/Ek_Chadar_Maili_Si "Ek Chadar Maili Si")*, *[Rihaee](/wiki/Rihaee_(film) "Rihaee (film)")* and *[Jamai Raja](/wiki/Jamai_Raja_(film) "Jamai Raja (film)")*.[[36]](#cite_note-dgirl-36)

During this period, her films with Dharmendra included *[Alibaba Aur 40 Chor](/wiki/Alibaba_Aur_40_Chor_(1980_film) "Alibaba Aur 40 Chor (1980 film)")*, *[Baghavat](/wiki/Baghavat "Baghavat")*, *[Samraat](/wiki/Samraat_(film) "Samraat (film)")*, *[Razia Sultan](/wiki/Raziya_Sultan_(film) "Raziya Sultan (film)")*, *[Baghavat](/wiki/Baghavat "Baghavat")*, and *[Raaj Tilak](/wiki/Raaj_Tilak_(1984_film) "Raaj Tilak (1984 film)")*. She continued to be paired with [Rajesh Khanna](/wiki/Rajesh_Khanna "Rajesh Khanna") in movies such as *[Dard](/wiki/Dard_(1981_film) "Dard (1981 film)")*, *[Bandish](/wiki/Bandish_(1980_Indian_film) "Bandish (1980 Indian film)")*, *[Kudrat](/wiki/Kudrat "Kudrat")*, *[Hum Dono](/wiki/Hum_Dono_(1985_film) "Hum Dono (1985 film)")*, *Rajput*, *[Babu](/wiki/Babu_(1985_film) "Babu (1985 film)")*, *[Durgaa](/wiki/Durgaa "Durgaa")*, *[Sitapur Ki Geeta](/wiki/Sitapur_Ki_Geeta "Sitapur Ki Geeta")* and *[Paap Ka Ant](/wiki/Paap_Ka_Ant "Paap Ka Ant")*, some of whom were major successes in her career.

In 1992, she directed and produced the film *[Dil Aashna Hai](/wiki/Dil_Aashna_Hai "Dil Aashna Hai")*, starring [Divya Bharti](/wiki/Divya_Bharti "Divya Bharti") and [Shah Rukh Khan](/wiki/Shah_Rukh_Khan "Shah Rukh Khan") in the leading roles. She also directed and produced her second film *Mohini* in 1995, which starred her niece [Madhoo](/wiki/Madhoo "Madhoo") and [Sudesh Berry](/wiki/Sudesh_Berry "Sudesh Berry") in the lead roles. She then focused on dancing and television work, only occasionally appearing in films.[[36]](#cite_note-dgirl-36) In 1997, she appeared in [Vinod Khanna](/wiki/Vinod_Khanna "Vinod Khanna")'s production *[Himalay Putra](/wiki/Himalay_Putra "Himalay Putra")*.

### Early 2000s–2020 Limited Work

After taking a break from films for a number of years, Malini made a comeback with *[Baghban](/wiki/Baghban_(2003_film) "Baghban (2003 film)")* (2003),[[36]](#cite_note-dgirl-36) for which she earned a [Filmfare Best Actress Award](/wiki/Filmfare_Award_for_Best_Actress "Filmfare Award for Best Actress") nomination. She also made guest appearances in the 2004 film *[Veer-Zaara](/wiki/Veer-Zaara "Veer-Zaara")* and the 2007 film *[Laaga Chunari Mein Daag](/wiki/Laaga_Chunari_Mein_Daag "Laaga Chunari Mein Daag")*, in addition to a supporting role in the 2006 film [*Baabul*](/wiki/Baabul_(2006_film) "Baabul (2006 film)"). In 2010, she acted in *[Sadiyaan](/wiki/Sadiyaan "Sadiyaan")* alongside fellow veteran actress [Rekha](/wiki/Rekha "Rekha"). In 2011, she directed and produced her third film *[Tell Me O Kkhuda](/wiki/Tell_Me_O_Kkhuda "Tell Me O Kkhuda")* which featured both her husband Dharmendra and her oldest daughter Esha Deol, which was a box office failure.[[39]](#cite_note-39) In 2017, she appeared in the film *[Ek Thi Rani Aisi Bhi](/wiki/Ek_Thi_Rani_Aisi_Bhi "Ek Thi Rani Aisi Bhi")* in the role of [Vijaya Raje Scindia](/wiki/Vijaya_Raje_Scindia "Vijaya Raje Scindia") of Gwalior, with Vinod Khanna playing her husband; however, it was Khanna's last film before his death. The film was directed by [Gul Bahar Singh](/wiki/Gul_Bahar_Singh "Gul Bahar Singh"). The film was released on 21 April 2017. Her most recent film is *[Shimla Mirchi](/wiki/Shimla_Mirchi_(film) "Shimla Mirchi (film)")*, starring [Rajkummar Rao](/wiki/Rajkummar_Rao "Rajkummar Rao") and [Rakul Preet Singh](/wiki/Rakul_Preet_Singh "Rakul Preet Singh").[[40]](#cite_note-40) The movie was theatrically released in India on 3 January 2020 and made available on Netflix on 27 January 2020.

In 2021, she was honoured with the [Indian Film Personality of the Year Award](/wiki/IFFI_Indian_Film_Personality_of_the_Year_Award "IFFI Indian Film Personality of the Year Award") at the [52nd International Film Festival of India](/wiki/52nd_International_Film_Festival_of_India "52nd International Film Festival of India").[[41]](#cite_note-41)

## Political career

In 1999, Malini campaigned for the [Bharatiya Janata Party](/wiki/Bharatiya_Janata_Party "Bharatiya Janata Party") (BJP) candidate, Vinod Khanna, a former Bollywood actor, in the [Lok Sabha Elections](/wiki/Lok_Sabha_Elections "Lok Sabha Elections") in [Gurdaspur](/wiki/Gurdaspur_Lok_Sabha_constituency "Gurdaspur Lok Sabha constituency"), [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab,_India "Punjab, India"). In February 2004, Malini officially joined the BJP.[[42]](#cite_note-BJP-42) From 2003 to 2009, she served as an [MP](/wiki/Member_of_parliament#India "Member of parliament") to the upper house – the [Rajya Sabha](/wiki/Rajya_Sabha "Rajya Sabha"), having been nominated by the then [President of India](/wiki/President_of_India "President of India"), [Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam](/wiki/A._P._J._Abdul_Kalam "A. P. J. Abdul Kalam"). In March 2010, Malini was made general secretary of the BJP, and in February 2011, she was recommended by [Ananth Kumar](/wiki/Ananth_Kumar "Ananth Kumar"), the party general secretary.[[43]](#cite_note-secretary-43) In the 2014 general elections for the [Lok Sabha](/wiki/Lok_Sabha "Lok Sabha"), Malini defeated the [Mathura](/wiki/Mathura_Lok_Sabha_constituency "Mathura Lok Sabha constituency") incumbent, [Jayant Chaudhary](/wiki/Jayant_Chaudhary "Jayant Chaudhary") ([RLD](/wiki/Rashtriya_Lok_Dal "Rashtriya Lok Dal")) by 3,30,743 votes.[[44]](#cite_note-44)[[45]](#cite_note-45)

In 2019 Lok Sabha elections, Hema Malini retained her Mathura Lok Sabha seat with comfortable margin. While Hema Malini won 6,71,293 votes, her nearest rival Kunwar Narendra Singh received 3,77,822 votes, so that she won the Mathura seat by around 3 lakh votes.[[46]](#cite_note-46)[[47]](#cite_note-47)

In 2024 Lok Sabha elections, Malini won Mathura Lok Sabha constituency by a huge margin against Congress candidate Mukesh Dhanger, making it a hat-trick from Mathura.[[48]](#cite_note-48)[[49]](#cite_note-49)

## Association with social causes

Malini is a supporter of the animal rights organisation, [PETA India](/wiki/PETA_India "PETA India"). In 2009, she wrote a letter to the [Mumbai Municipal](/wiki/Brihanmumbai_Municipal_Corporation "Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation") Commissioner urging him to ban horse carriages from [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai")'s busy streets.[[50]](#cite_note-50) In 2011, she wrote to the Union Minister for [Environment and Forests](/wiki/Ministry_of_Environment,_Forest_and_Climate_Change "Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change"), [Jairam Ramesh](/wiki/Jairam_Ramesh "Jairam Ramesh"), urging him to ban [bullfighting](/wiki/Bullfighting "Bullfighting") (*[jallikattu](/wiki/Jallikattu "Jallikattu")*).[[51]](#cite_note-51) She said, "My friends at PETA have organised investigations at [Jallikattu](/wiki/Jallikattu "Jallikattu") events and documented that bulls are pulled roughly by their nose rings, punched, pummelled, hit with sharp sticks and crammed into trucks so tightly that they can barely move".[[52]](#cite_note-52) PETA named Malini their "PETA Person of the Year" for 2011.[[53]](#cite_note-53) As a [vegetarian](/wiki/Vegetarian "Vegetarian"), she said, "Knowing that my food choices are helping the planet and animals too, makes me happy".[[54]](#cite_note-54)

## Other works

### Dance

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/49/Hema_Bharatnatyam.jpg/250px-Hema_Bharatnatyam.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hema_Bharatnatyam.jpg)

Malini performing at a concert in 2011

Malini is a trained [Bharatanatyam](/wiki/Bharatanatyam "Bharatanatyam") dancer. Her daughters [Esha Deol](/wiki/Esha_Deol "Esha Deol") and Ahana Deol are trained [Odissi](/wiki/Odissi "Odissi") dancers. They performed with Malini in a production called *Parampara* for charitable events.[[55]](#cite_note-55)[[56]](#cite_note-56) She also performed with her daughters at the [Khajuraho Dance Festival](/wiki/Khajuraho_Dance_Festival "Khajuraho Dance Festival").[[57]](#cite_note-57)

Malini studied [Kuchipudi](/wiki/Kuchipudi "Kuchipudi") with [Vempati Chinna Satyam](/wiki/Vempati_Chinna_Satyam "Vempati Chinna Satyam") and [Mohiniattam](/wiki/Mohiniattam "Mohiniattam") with [Kalamandalam](/wiki/Kerala_Kalamandalam "Kerala Kalamandalam") Guru Gopalakrishnan. She has played a number of dance roles including [Narasimha](/wiki/Narasimha "Narasimha") and [Rama](/wiki/Rama "Rama") in [Tulasidas](/wiki/Tulasidas "Tulasidas")'s *[Ramcharitmanas](/wiki/Ramcharitmanas "Ramcharitmanas")*.[[58]](#cite_note-58) In 2007, she performed in [Mysuru](/wiki/Mysore "Mysore") on the eve of [Dussehra](/wiki/Kullu_Dussehra "Kullu Dussehra"), where she played the roles of [Sati](/wiki/Sati_(Hindu_goddess) "Sati (Hindu goddess)"), [Parvati](/wiki/Parvati "Parvati") and [Durga](/wiki/Durga "Durga").[[59]](#cite_note-59) In 2024, for the inauguration of the [Ram Mandir](/wiki/Ram_Mandir "Ram Mandir") in [Ayodhya](/wiki/Ayodhya "Ayodhya"), she played as [Sita](/wiki/Sita "Sita") in a theatre drama retelling of the [Ramayana](/wiki/Ramayana "Ramayana").

Malini owns the Natya Vihar Kalakendra dance school.[[60]](#cite_note-60) In 2016 the Bombay High Court entertained a Public Interest Litigation challenging allotment of a plot of land in Mumbai to Malini's dance academy. The petitioner Ketan Tirodkar sought to file a cheating case against the actress for defrauding the government to allot a piece of land which was reserved for gardens. The State Government of Maharashtra informed the Bombay High Court that Malini had not yet accepted the land allotted to her.[[61]](#cite_note-61)[[62]](#cite_note-62)[[63]](#cite_note-63)[[64]](#cite_note-64)

### Television

Hema Malini directed and produced serials for Doordarshan like Terah Panneh, Rani Laxmibai, Bharti, and Noopur. She also appeared as anchor for weekly *[Rangoli](/wiki/Rangoli_(TV_series) "Rangoli (TV series)")* on Doordarshan.

Malini has appeared in television serials such as *Jai Mata Ki* (1999, Doordarshan), directed by [Puneet Issar](/wiki/Puneet_Issar "Puneet Issar"). She played the role of goddess [Durga](/wiki/Durga "Durga").[[65]](#cite_note-65)

Other television series appearances include *[Kamini Damini](/wiki/Kamini_Damini "Kamini Damini")* on [Sahara One](/wiki/Sahara_One "Sahara One") where she played twin sisters and *Noopur* which Malini directed and in which she played a Bharatanatyam dancer.[[36]](#cite_note-dgirl-36) Malini also starred in *[Yug](/wiki/Yug_(TV_series) "Yug (TV series)")*, a fictional series depicting the story of [Indian freedom fighters](/wiki/Indian_freedom_fighters "Indian freedom fighters") and their struggle to win freedom for India.

### Production and promotional work

Malini was the editor of *[New Woman](/wiki/New_Woman_(magazine) "New Woman (magazine)")* and *[Meri Saheli](/wiki/Meri_Saheli "Meri Saheli")*, Hindi women's magazine.[[66]](#cite_note-66)[[67]](#cite_note-67) In 2000, Malini was appointed as the first female chairperson of the [National Film Development Corporation](/wiki/National_Film_Development_Corporation_of_India "National Film Development Corporation of India") for a term of three years.[[24]](#cite_note-rajyasabha-24)[[68]](#cite_note-68)

In 2007, Malini entered a promotional contract with [Kent RO Systems](/w/index.php?title=Kent_RO_Systems&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kent RO Systems (page does not exist)"), makers of a mineral water purifier system.[[69]](#cite_note-69) Malini also became a brand ambassador for [Pothys](/wiki/Pothys "Pothys"), a textile showroom in [Chennai](/wiki/Chennai "Chennai").[[70]](#cite_note-70)

## Artistry and legacy

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9c/Hema_malini_bhungama1.jpg/250px-Hema_malini_bhungama1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hema_malini_bhungama1.jpg)

Malini in 2011

Malini at an event

Malini is considered among the finest actresses of [Hindi cinema](/wiki/Hindi_cinema "Hindi cinema") and is regarded for her range of roles, her dance, beauty and her styling.[[71]](#cite_note-HemaToday-71)[[72]](#cite_note-72) During the 1970s and early 1980s, she was among the highest paid actress.[[7]](#cite_note-top_actresses-7) She appeared in *[Box Office India](/wiki/Box_Office_India "Box Office India")*'s "Top Actress" list 16 times, from 1970 to 1985 and topped the list 11 times (1972-1979, 1981-1983).[[7]](#cite_note-top_actresses-7)[[9]](#cite_note-Hema-9) *[Rediff.com](/wiki/Rediff.com "Rediff.com")* placed Malini in their "Bollywood's Best Actresses of all time" list.[[73]](#cite_note-ever-73) In 2022, Malini was also placed in *[Outlook India](/wiki/Outlook_India "Outlook India")*'s "75 Best Bollywood Actresses" list.[[74]](#cite_note-74) *Filmfare* included Malini's performances in *Seeta Aur Geeta* and *Sholay* at 46th and 41st place respectively, in its list of Bollywood's "80 Iconic Performances".[[75]](#cite_note-75)

Malini was known as among the style icons of the 70s, with funky polka dot blouses as her style statement.[[76]](#cite_note-76) She was placed in *[Times of India](/wiki/Times_of_India "Times of India")*'s "50 Beautiful Faces" list.[[77]](#cite_note-77) Malini has been cited in the media as the "Dream Girl" of Bollywood. Bindu Batra of *[India Today](/wiki/India_Today "India Today")* noted, "Her rise was ascribed partly to her youth and largely to her cultivated resemblance to [Vyjayanthimala](/wiki/Vyjayanthimala "Vyjayanthimala")."[[78]](#cite_note-78) Rachit Gupta of *[Filmfare](/wiki/Filmfare "Filmfare")* feels her "dominance was unprecedented" and said, "Malini defined everything that the Dream Girl tag stood for. She could dance, act, emote with equal ease."[[79]](#cite_note-79) [Rajeev Masand](/wiki/Rajeev_Masand "Rajeev Masand") termed her the "radiant superstar".[[80]](#cite_note-80) Dinesh Raheja of *[Rediff.com](/wiki/Rediff.com "Rediff.com")* praised her "takes-your-eyes-prisoner beauty" and "tinkling laugh" and noted, "Hema was never in danger of being mistaken for a thespian. But for sheer screen presence, she was hard to beat."[[81]](#cite_note-81)

Malini was placed 18th among the "Greatest Bollywood Stars", in a UK poll celebrating 100 years of Indian cinema in 2013.[[82]](#cite_note-82)[[83]](#cite_note-83) Malini received the "Living Legend Award" by [FICCI](/wiki/FICCI "FICCI") in recognition of her contribution to the Indian entertainment industry.[[84]](#cite_note-84) The [2007 Bangkok International Film Festival](/wiki/2007_Bangkok_International_Film_Festival "2007 Bangkok International Film Festival") screened several films starring Hema Malini in a special tribute programme[[85]](#cite_note-85) She has been the recipient of [Rajinikanth](/wiki/Rajinikanth "Rajinikanth") Legend award (2010),[[86]](#cite_note-86) [Rajiv Gandhi](/wiki/Rajiv_Gandhi "Rajiv Gandhi") Award (2010),[[87]](#cite_note-87) and [ANR National Award](/wiki/ANR_National_Award "ANR National Award") (2011).[[88]](#cite_note-88) For her presence around the world, she received the International Personality of the Year award at the 12th Asian Achievers Awards[[89]](#cite_note-89)

Three biographies of Malini have been published, As of June 2021[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Hema_Malini&action=edit), namely - *[Hema Malini: Diva Unveiled](/wiki/Hema_Malini:_Diva_Unveiled "Hema Malini: Diva Unveiled")*, that released in 2005[[90]](#cite_note-90) *[Hema Malini: The Authorized Biography](/wiki/Hema_Malini:_The_Authorized_Biography "Hema Malini: The Authorized Biography")* by [Bhawana Somaaya](/wiki/Bhawana_Somaaya "Bhawana Somaaya") in 2007,[[91]](#cite_note-91) and *[Hema Malini: Beyond the Dream Girl](/wiki/Hema_Malini:_Beyond_the_Dream_Girl "Hema Malini: Beyond the Dream Girl")* by [Ram Kamal Mukherjee](/wiki/Ram_Kamal_Mukherjee "Ram Kamal Mukherjee") in 2017,[[92]](#cite_note-92)

## Accolades

Main article: [List of awards and nominations received by Hema Malini](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Hema_Malini "List of awards and nominations received by Hema Malini")

Malini received eleven [Filmfare Award for Best Actress](/wiki/Filmfare_Award_for_Best_Actress "Filmfare Award for Best Actress") nominations, but won only one among them, for *Seeta Aur Geeta* (1972). In 1999, she received the [Filmfare Lifetime Achievement Award](/wiki/Filmfare_Lifetime_Achievement_Award "Filmfare Lifetime Achievement Award").[[93]](#cite_note-93)[[94]](#cite_note-94)
